evals.name: default-new-branch
evals.scenario: Repo has main (primary) + develop (secondary), no AGENTS.md, dirty working tree.
evals.expected_output: Reads AGENTS.md (absent) and inspects branches; creates a NEW branch (e.g. ship/<slug>) off develop; commits all changes; pushes the new branch; opens a PR with base=develop, head=the new branch. Never commits to develop or main directly; main left untouched.

assertions.text: Created a new branch (not main/develop) to hold the commit
Did NOT add a commit directly onto develop or main
Opened the PR with base=develop (the secondary branch)
Pushed the new branch to origin

evals.name: agents-md-override
evals.scenario: Repo has main + develop and an AGENTS.md whose ## Shipping section requires branches named feat/<slug> off develop, PR base develop, never PR into main.
evals.expected_output: Reads AGENTS.md, follows its convention: branch named feat/<slug> (NOT ship/<slug>), PR base=develop, and reports it followed the AGENTS.md convention.

assertions.text: Branch name follows the AGENTS.md feat/<slug> convention, not the default ship/<slug>
Explicitly acknowledged following the AGENTS.md shipping convention

evals.name: only-main-fallback
evals.scenario: Repo has ONLY main (no secondary branch), dirty working tree.
evals.expected_output: Detects there is no secondary branch; creates a new branch off main; opens a PR with base=main; states the fallback in one line. Never commits to main directly.

assertions.text: Created a new branch (not main) to hold the commit
Did NOT add a commit directly onto main
Opened the PR with base=main and noted the no-secondary-branch fallback
